edges.description: Loss of the structural subunit yields a catalytically deficient enzyme.
edges.source: COX6B1 Loss and Defective Complex IV Assembly
edges.target: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Energy deficit in the CNS produces the infantile encephalomyopathy.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Energy failure in cardiomyocytes drives hypertrophic cardiomyopathy in some patients.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

nodes.description: Biallelic COX6B1 variants cause loss of a nuclear-encoded structural subunit of cytochrome c oxidase, destabilizing the holoenzyme and impairing assembly of a functional Complex IV. Unlike most nuclear COX defects, which affect assembly factors or metallochaperones, the lesion here is in a structural subunit itself.
nodes.id: COX6B1 Loss and Defective Complex IV Assembly

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: Severe infantile encephalomyopathy caused by a mutation in COX6B1, a nucleus-encoded subunit of cytochrome c oxidase.
evidence.snippet: We report a disease-associated mutation in one such subunit, COX6B1.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial complex IV deficiency, caused by mutated COX6B1, is associated with encephalomyopathy, hydrocephalus and cardiomyopathy.
evidence.snippet: enzymatic activity was undetectable in muscle and fibroblasts, was severely decreased in lymphocytes and the COX6B1 protein was barely detectable in patient's muscle mitochondria

nodes.description: Severe infantile encephalomyopathy, the cardinal presentation of COX6B1-related COX deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial complex IV deficiency, caused by mutated COX6B1, is associated with encephalomyopathy, hydrocephalus and cardiomyopathy.
evidence.snippet: report a case of isolated COX deficiency manifesting with encephalomyopathy, hydrocephalus and hypertropic cardiomyopathy due to a missense p.R20C mutation in the COX6B1 gene

evidence.reference_title: A novel homozygous pathogenic missense variant in COX6B1: Further delineation of the phenotype.
evidence.snippet: a patient with a novel homozygous pathogenic variant in COX6B1 who presented acutely with severe encephalomyopathy associated with an infection

nodes.description: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy reported in COX6B1-related COX deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial complex IV deficiency, caused by mutated COX6B1, is associated with encephalomyopathy, hydrocephalus and cardiomyopathy.
evidence.snippet: report a case of isolated COX deficiency manifesting with encephalomyopathy, hydrocephalus and hypertropic cardiomyopathy due to a missense p.R20C mutation in the COX6B1 gene

nodes.description: Deficient Complex IV blocks electron transfer from cytochrome c to oxygen and proton pumping, collapsing oxidative ATP synthesis and preferentially injuring high-energy tissues (brain, heart, skeletal muscle).
nodes.id: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, cytochrome c to oxygen

evidence.reference_title: Fatal infantile cardioencephalomyopathy with COX deficiency and mutations in SCO2, a COX assembly gene.
evidence.snippet: Mammalian cytochrome c oxidase (COX) catalyses the transfer of reducing equivalents from cytochrome c to molecular oxygen and pumps protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane.
